docs.name: SmolVLM (vision-language)
docs.blurb: Describe, answer questions about, and reason over an image with a tiny VLM.
docs.unlocks: In-browser visual Q&A, alt-text, and screenshot understanding with zero server cost.

docs.name: SmolVLM2 (multi-image / video)
docs.blurb: Reason across several images or sampled video frames at once — what changed, how they compare, what happened over time.
docs.unlocks: In-browser video-moment triage, before/after diffs, and temporal visual reasoning with zero server cost.

docs.name: Whisper (speech-to-text)
docs.blurb: Transcribe speech from the mic or a file, with word timestamps.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline dictation, captions, and voice commands — audio never leaves the device.

docs.name: Kokoro (text-to-speech)
docs.blurb: High-quality speech synthesis with multiple voices, fully local.
docs.unlocks: Natural narration and accessibility read-aloud with no API keys or per-character billing.

docs.name: SpeechT5 (text-to-speech)
docs.blurb: Speaker-conditioned speech synthesis: the voice is a swappable 512-dim x-vector, with an auto-loaded HiFi-GAN vocoder.
docs.unlocks: Pick, swap, or blend voices at runtime from a library of speaker embeddings — voice-cloning-adjacent TTS with no cloud bill.

docs.name: Qwen2.5 0.5B (chat LLM)
docs.blurb: A small instruct LLM that streams tokens locally with sampling controls.
docs.unlocks: Private chat, structured extraction, and on-device agents with no inference bill.

docs.name: Llama 3.2 1B (WebLLM chat)
docs.blurb: A capable 1B chat model running on MLC's WebGPU engine, fully local.
docs.unlocks: Private ChatGPT-style assistants, on-device RAG, and offline agents with no server or API bill.

docs.name: Llama 3.2 3B (WebLLM chat)
docs.blurb: The bigger Llama 3.2 chat model (3.21B) on MLC's WebGPU engine — more reasoning headroom than the 1B, fully local.
docs.unlocks: Reliable on-device tool-routing, structured output, long-form persona work, and grounded RAG with a stronger reader — no server or API bill.

docs.name: Gemma 2 2B (WebLLM chat)
docs.blurb: Google's Gemma 2 2B — the catalogue's most capable chat model — running on MLC's WebGPU engine, fully local.
docs.unlocks: Private, higher-capability chat: on-device reasoning, structured extraction, personas, and grounded RAG with no server or API bill.

docs.name: CLIP (zero-shot image)
docs.blurb: Classify an image against ANY labels you type — no training.
docs.unlocks: Custom, on-the-fly image moderation, tagging, and search with labels you invent at runtime.

docs.name: Depth Anything v2 (monocular depth)
docs.blurb: Turn any single photo into a depth map, live.
docs.unlocks: 3D parallax, background blur, and AR occlusion from an ordinary 2D image.

docs.name: Marigold (diffusion depth)
docs.blurb: Diffusion-based monocular depth: a Stable-Diffusion-style denoising UNet iteratively refines a depth latent — distinct from single-pass regression depth (Depth Anything / DPT / DepthPro).
docs.unlocks: Sharper, detail-preserving monocular depth via a latent-diffusion denoising loop.
docs.blockedReason: Marigold is a latent-DIFFUSION pipeline, not a transformers.js model, and transformers.js has no diffusion depth pipeline. Verified 2026-07-19: (1) All prs-eth/marigold-depth-* repos are library:diffusers, safetensors-only, and have NO root config.json (HTTP 404) — only model_index.json declaring a MarigoldDepthPipeline (scheduler + text_encoder + unet + vae components), so pipeline("depth-estimation", "prs-eth/marigold-depth-v1-1") fails at AutoConfig load. (2) transformers.js 3.7.5 AND 4.2.0 register NO marigold class (grep of both dist bundles: zero matches); the depth-estimation pipeline MODEL_FOR_DEPTH_ESTIMATION_MAPPING supports only single-pass regression heads (depth_anything, depth_pro, dpt, glpn, sapiens) — there is no AutoencoderKL / UNet2DConditionModel / DDIMScheduler denoising loop in the library. (3) The one ONNX export (julienkay/Marigold) is a diffusers OnnxStableDiffusionPipeline (model_index.json _class_name:OnnxStableDiffusionPipeline, DDIMScheduler, separate unet/vae_encoder/vae_decoder/text_encoder ONNX) whose UNet alone is 3.46 GB (unet/weights.pb) and requires a hand-written VAE-encode -> iterative DDIM denoise -> VAE-decode loop — not the transformers.js pipeline()/createModelLoader architecture this showcase runs, and not browser-feasible at that size. Not shipped rather than relabel a single-pass regression depth model (Depth Anything / DPT / DepthPro — already built) as diffusion-based Marigold. Unblocks if an onnx-community browser-feasible Marigold export lands AND transformers.js gains a diffusion depth pipeline (or the showcase adds an onnxruntime-web diffusion runtime).

docs.name: DepthPro (sharp metric-scale depth)
docs.blurb: Apple DepthPro: sharp, boundary-accurate, metric-scale monocular depth — distinct from Depth Anything relative maps. A large WebGPU model.
docs.unlocks: Metric-scale distances and clean depth boundaries from one photo — real measurement, crisp cut-outs and AR occlusion — on-device via WebGPU.

docs.name: RMBG-1.4 (background removal)
docs.blurb: Cut the subject out of any photo, entirely in the browser.
docs.unlocks: Instant private background removal for avatars, product shots, and stickers — no upload.

docs.name: MODNet (portrait matting)
docs.blurb: Trimap-free portrait matting that keeps individual strands of hair.
docs.unlocks: Believable cut-outs and virtual backgrounds with soft hair edges — the matte a hard background remover cannot produce, on-device.

docs.name: DETR (object detection)
docs.blurb: Detect and box objects in an image or webcam feed.
docs.unlocks: On-device counting, redaction, and scene understanding with no camera stream leaving the page.

docs.name: RT-DETR (real-time object detection)
docs.blurb: A real-time, NMS-free DETR: one clean box per object, fast enough for a live webcam.
docs.unlocks: Frame-rate object detection in a tab — real-time counting and camera-aware UX — at a tenth of DETR's size, no server.

docs.name: D-FINE (SOTA real-time object detection)
docs.blurb: A state-of-the-art real-time detector that reframes box regression as a distribution it iteratively refines (FDR + GO-LSD) — NMS-free, and more accurate than RT-DETR at the same speed.
docs.unlocks: Higher-accuracy frame-rate object detection in a tab — SOTA real-time boxes at a ~10 MB download, no server.

docs.name: OWLv2 (open-vocabulary detection)
docs.blurb: Detect any object you describe in free text — no fixed class list, no retraining.
docs.unlocks: Bespoke on-device detection from a text box: custom inventory, inspection, and visual search with nothing uploaded.

docs.name: OWLViT (open-vocabulary detection)
docs.blurb: The original open-vocabulary detector — box anything you can describe in text.
docs.unlocks: Bespoke on-device detection from a text box, lighter than OWLv2: custom inventory, inspection, and visual search with nothing uploaded.

docs.name: Grounding DINO (phrase grounding)
docs.blurb: Ground a natural-language caption to boxes — write "a cat. a remote." and it finds each phrase.
docs.unlocks: Describe-to-find detection and grounded captions from a sentence: inspection, visual search, and referring-phrase localisation, nothing uploaded.

docs.name: Swin2SR (2× super-resolution)
docs.blurb: Upscale a small or compressed image 2× and reconstruct real detail.
docs.unlocks: Private, free, offline image enhancement — sharper thumbnails, rescued compressions, restored photos — with no upscaling service.

docs.name: SlimSAM (segment anything)
docs.blurb: Click a point, get a precise mask of whatever you clicked.
docs.unlocks: Interactive cut-out, smart selection, and annotation tools without a server round-trip.

docs.name: SAM 2.1 (segment anything 2)
docs.blurb: Click a point or drag a box; get three high-quality candidate masks with IoU scores.
docs.unlocks: Promptable, refinable segmentation (points + boxes) for cut-out, measurement and annotation — the Hiera-backbone successor to SAM, on-device.

docs.name: all-MiniLM-L6 (text embeddings)
docs.blurb: Turn text into vectors for semantic search and clustering.
docs.unlocks: Private semantic search, dedup, and recommendation that runs in the tab with no vector DB.

docs.name: GTE-small (text embeddings)
docs.blurb: A 33M-param embedder that ranks with the big models on MTEB — mean-pooled, no query prefix.
docs.unlocks: Top-tier semantic search, clustering, and nearest-label classification in the tab, with MiniLM/BGE/GTE compared side by side.

docs.name: DistilBERT SST-2 (sentiment)
docs.blurb: Score text as positive/negative, with the token-level attributions.
docs.unlocks: Live, private moderation and feedback triage as the user types — no PII leaves the device.

docs.name: News topic classification (10-class head)
docs.blurb: Auto-tag any headline or article onto one of 10 news desks — Sports, Business, Politics, Science… — with a dedicated topic head in one forward pass.
docs.unlocks: Instant, private newsroom auto-tagging and topic-routed feeds — a fixed-taxonomy alternative to zero-shot, at ~13 MB and no server round-trip.

docs.name: Formality detection (register)
docs.blurb: Score any text as formal or informal register — a stylistic axis orthogonal to sentiment — with a live formality meter and word-level register cues.
docs.unlocks: Private, in-browser tone checking: keep client emails formal, flag the one casual line in a draft, or paraphrase to shift register — no text leaving the device.

docs.name: FinBERT (financial sentiment)
docs.blurb: A BERT domain-adapted on a financial corpus and fine-tuned on the Financial PhraseBank: scores finance text as positive / negative / neutral the way a market participant reads it, not a movie-review model.
docs.unlocks: Private, on-device triage of headlines, earnings lines and analyst notes by tone — signal not advice — including a neutral class that general sentiment models lack.

docs.name: DistilBERT SQuAD (question answering)
docs.blurb: Extract the exact answer span for a question from a passage.
docs.unlocks: Grounded answers from your own documents — FAQ bots and on-device RAG that can't hallucinate facts.

docs.name: Multilingual QA (XLM-RoBERTa SQuAD2)
docs.blurb: Extract the exact answer span for a question from a passage — in ~100 languages, one model.
docs.unlocks: Multilingual grounded QA: FAQ search, cross-lingual lookup and on-device RAG over documents in any language, no translation step.

docs.name: MS MARCO MiniLM (cross-encoder reranker)
docs.blurb: Rerank passages by reading each query–passage pair together for real relevance.
docs.unlocks: Two-stage search and better RAG — cross-encoder precision on a shortlist, fully on-device.

docs.name: STS-B similarity (cross-encoder regression)
docs.blurb: Score how similar two sentences mean on a graded 0–5 scale by reading them together (cross-encoder).
docs.unlocks: Calibrated semantic similarity for dedup, paraphrase detection and answer grading with a real threshold — reading meaning, not word overlap.

docs.name: DeBERTa-v3 (zero-shot classification)
docs.blurb: Classify text against labels you invent at run time — no training.
docs.unlocks: Intent routing, ticket triage, and flexible tagging with custom labels — edit categories, never retrain.

docs.name: Florence-2 (vision foundation)
docs.blurb: One model, many vision tasks: caption, detect, segment, OCR — via task prompts.
docs.unlocks: A single tiny download that does captioning, grounding, OCR and region description on-device.

docs.name: Florence-2-large (vision foundation)
docs.blurb: The larger 0.77B Florence-2 checkpoint: same task-prompt API as the base, sharper captions, detection and OCR.
docs.unlocks: A single on-device model for caption/detect/OCR/segment at higher accuracy than the base — trade download size and GPU memory for quality.

docs.name: DistilBART CNN (summarization)
docs.blurb: Summarize long text into a short abstractive summary, streaming as it generates.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline TL;DRs of docs, emails, and transcripts — no API key or per-call billing.

docs.name: LongT5 TGlobal base (summarization)
docs.blurb: Long-context gist summarizer with transient-global attention and Pegasus-style gap-sentence pretraining; a distinct family from BART. Base checkpoint, so gists lean extractive.
docs.unlocks: Headlines and TL;DRs for long documents that overflow a short-context summarizer — private, offline, no per-call billing.

docs.name: FLAN-T5-small (instruction seq2seq)
docs.blurb: One instruction-tuned encoder-decoder that classifies, answers from context, and summarizes from a plain instruction.
docs.unlocks: A universal on-device text tool and offline RAG answerer — swap the instruction, not the model; no server or API key.

docs.name: M2M100 418M (translation)
docs.blurb: Translate directly between any of 100 languages — no English pivot — fully on-device.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline translation and localisation drafts across 100 languages with no server.

docs.name: BERT base (fill-mask)
docs.blurb: Hide a word behind [MASK] and BERT ranks what belongs there — with the exact logits.
docs.unlocks: Private on-device autocomplete, knowledge probing and bias auditing with no prompt engineering or API bill.

docs.name: ModernBERT (fill-mask)
docs.blurb: A 2024 encoder — RoPE, GeGLU, alternating global/local attention, 8192-token context — filling [MASK] with the exact logits.
docs.unlocks: Long-document masked prediction, knowledge/code probing and a modern encoder backbone, all on-device.

docs.name: bge-small-en-v1.5 (sentence similarity)
docs.blurb: Retrieval-grade sentence embeddings — rank text by meaning, stronger than MiniLM.
docs.unlocks: On-device semantic search, dedup, clustering, and retrieve-then-rerank with no vector DB or API bill.

docs.name: GPT-2

docs.unlocks: A legible view of autoregressive generation (greedy vs temperature/top-k sampling); offline text completion/autocomplete; a teaching tool for how LLMs actually write; a generation stage for local pipelines.

docs.name: NSFW detector (ViT)
docs.blurb: Screen an image for NSFW content locally: safe/NSFW probability plus a clear 'safe to display?' verdict.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline upload gates and avatar screening — content-safety filtering with no image ever uploaded.

docs.name: toxic-bert (content moderation)
docs.blurb: Score a comment for toxicity across six independent labels (toxic, obscene, threat, insult, identity hate) with a moderation threshold.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline content-moderation triage — flag hostile text client-side before it's posted, no comment sent to a server.

docs.name: twitter-roberta-hate (protected-group hate)
docs.blurb: Detect hate speech aimed at a protected characteristic and name which one — gender, race, sexuality, religion, origin, disability, age — as a softmax over 8 classes. Narrower than toxicity; an imperfect signal, not a verdict.
docs.unlocks: Private, on-device protected-group hate detection that also names the targeted group, so a moderation queue can route and prioritise correctly without sending text to a server.

docs.name: hate_speech_en (offensive-language severity)
docs.blurb: Rate a comment on a four-step severity ladder — acceptable, inappropriate, offensive, violent — with one softmax. A broader net than hate or toxicity: catches profanity, insults, and hostility, framed as a moderation signal not a verdict.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline comment-queue triage by offensiveness severity, entirely client-side — sort a stream into flag/allow before it is posted, no comment sent to a server.

docs.unlocks: Flagship-grade, 99-language transcription and speech-to-English translation on-device — private, offline, and free of per-minute cost.

docs.name: BGE-reranker (multilingual cross-encoder)
docs.blurb: A multilingual cross-encoder reranker from BAAI (XLM-RoBERTa backbone): reads a query and a passage together and emits one relevance logit, ranking passages across languages.
docs.unlocks: Two-stage search and RAG that works across languages from one model — retrieve a shortlist fast, then rerank it for precision, even when the query and the best answer are in different languages.

docs.name: mxbai-rerank-xsmall (DeBERTa-v2 cross-encoder reranker)
docs.blurb: mixedbread’s DeBERTa-v2 cross-encoder reranker: reads a query and a passage together and emits one relevance logit.
docs.unlocks: Precise two-stage RAG and search reranking on-device, with a different backbone (DeBERTa-v2 disentangled attention) than the BERT/RoBERTa rerankers.

docs.name: Jina reranker (tiny cross-encoder)
docs.blurb: A tiny 4-layer English cross-encoder (~33 MB) built for speed: reads a query and passage together and emits one relevance logit, reranking a shortlist in milliseconds on a CPU.
docs.unlocks: The precision stage of search running fully on-device — rerank a retriever's shortlist inside a private RAG loop with no server, no inference bill, and no data leaving the browser.

docs.name: BART-large-MNLI (zero-shot classification)
docs.blurb: THE canonical zero-shot classifier — BART-large fine-tuned on MultiNLI. Classify text against labels you invent, no training.
docs.unlocks: Reference-grade intent routing, triage and multi-label tagging with custom labels; contrast a 406M backbone against tiny DeBERTa on the same input to decide what you actually need.

docs.name: Keyphrase extraction (token-classification)
docs.blurb: Extract the key phrases from a document as highlighted B/I spans — distinct from NER token-classification.
docs.unlocks: Auto-tagging, SEO, and document indexing from on-device keyphrase spans.
docs.blockedReason: No token-classification keyphrase model ships a browser-loadable ONNX export. Verified 2026-07-19: the KBIR/DistilBERT keyphrase families (Xenova/keyphrase-extraction-kbir-inspec + Xenova/kbir-inspec do not exist → HF 401; ml6team/keyphrase-extraction-kbir-inspec and ml6team/keyphrase-extraction-distilbert-* exist but are PyTorch-only, no onnx/ folder). A scan of 151 keyphrase/keyword repos found ONLY two with any .onnx: gmarcFG/tech-keywords-extractor-onnx and WiseIntelligence/cnmoro-ptt5_...-Optimum-ONNX — both are seq2seq GENERATION models (BART/T5 text2text), NOT token-classification, so they cannot produce the per-token B/I/O tags this page requires. The English one (gmarcFG) was confirmed to load and run in-browser (transformers.js 3.7.5, q8) but its quantized ONNX is degraded to hallucinated garbage output (e.g. 'EEI', 'OOES', 'LORBRIN'; repo ships only quantized ONNX, no higher-precision fallback), and it is architecturally redundant with existing BART/T5 seq2seq pages. Not shipped rather than mislabel a generator or plain NER as keyphrase span extraction. Unblocks when a token-classification keyphrase model (e.g. a KBIR/DistilBERT export via Xenova/onnx-community) ships an ONNX build.

docs.name: Bio_ClinicalBERT (clinical fill-mask)
docs.blurb: BERT trained on MIMIC-III clinical notes — masked-word prediction with a medical education, contrasted head-to-head with general BERT.
docs.unlocks: Private, on-device clinical autocomplete, term/abbreviation scoring, and a measurable domain-vs-general gap — no prompt engineering, no API bill.

docs.name: Legal-BERT (legal fill-mask)
docs.tierRationale: distinct but the task already has built demos — build after the high tier.
docs.blurb: Legal-domain masked LM — [MASK] completion on contract/statute text, contrasted head-to-head with general BERT. Distinct legal label domain vs the general/clinical fill-mask.
docs.unlocks: Private, on-device legal-clause autocomplete and a measurable legal-vs-general domain gap.
docs.blockedReason: No legal-domain fill-mask model ships a browser-loadable ONNX with a working masked-LM head. Verified 2026-07-19 (transformers.js 3.7.5, q8/fp32, headless Chrome WASM): (1) Xenova/legal-bert-base-uncased does not exist (HF 401). (2) The canonical Legal-BERT fill-mask models are PyTorch-only, no ONNX export: nlpaueb/legal-bert-base-uncased, nlpaueb/legal-bert-small-uncased, pile-of-law/legalbert-large-1.7M-2, casehold/legalbert, law-ai/InLegalBERT. (3) The only legal-BERT ONNX repos with a transformers.js standard layout are broken as masked LMs: adalbianco/legal_bert_onnx exports a BertForPreTraining graph whose sole output tensor is the next-sentence head — AutoModelForMaskedLM returns logits of shape [1,2] (no vocab dimension), and the fill-mask pipeline throws 'IndexError: index N out of bounds for dimension with size 2'; aazharnaeem/legal-bert-onnx returns a logits output that is not a usable tensor (accessing .dims throws; no vocab logits). (4) philGyamfi/legalbert.onnx has no config.json (HF 404) so transformers.js cannot determine the model type and will not load. (5) sekarkrishna/legalbert-int8 is a feature-extraction export (no MLM head). No onnx-community or Xenova legal-domain fill-mask export exists (pipeline_tag=fill-mask + onnx filter returns empty). Feature-extraction-only / NSP-only exports are NOT fill-mask. Not shipped rather than mislabel general BERT or a broken head as Legal-BERT. Unblocks when a legal-domain fill-mask model ships a browser-loadable ONNX with a masked-LM (prediction) head (e.g. a Xenova/onnx-community export of nlpaueb/legal-bert-base-uncased).

docs.name: SciBERT (scientific fill-mask)
docs.blurb: SciBERT's scientific-vocabulary masked LM — distinct from general BERT via its 31k scivocab tokenizer.
docs.unlocks: Scientific-domain masked-word prediction with a science-native vocabulary.
docs.blockedReason: No SciBERT scivocab masked-LM (fill-mask) runs in the browser today. Verified 2026-07-19 (transformers.js 3.7.5, q8, headless Chrome): allenai/scibert_scivocab_uncased ships no ONNX (PyTorch-only). The only SciBERT scivocab ONNX exports — Xenova/scibert_scivocab_uncased and onnx-community/scibert_scivocab_uncased-ONNX — are FEATURE-EXTRACTION exports with no masked-LM head: pipeline('fill-mask', ...) throws "Cannot read properties of undefined (reading '0')" because the model emits last_hidden_state, not vocab logits (config architectures:null; the scivocab vocab_size 31090 confirms it is the SciBERT encoder without the cls/predictions head). The nearest biomedical fill-mask ONNX that DOES ship an MLM head, onnx-community/biobert_v1.1_pubmed-ONNX (BertForMaskedLM), loads but its head is broken — a near-uniform distribution (top prob ~0.3-0.5%, tokens like 'coordinate','##bal') at BOTH q8 and fp32, so it is unusable, not a quantisation artifact. The domain fill-mask slot is instead served by the runnable, verified onnx-community/Bio_ClinicalBERT-ONNX (slug bio-clinicalbert). Unblocks when a SciBERT scivocab model ships a browser-loadable ONNX WITH a masked-LM head.

docs.name: SigLIP (zero-shot image)
docs.blurb: Classify an image against ANY labels — like CLIP but with independent per-label sigmoid scores.
docs.unlocks: Multi-label tagging and honest moderation with a real 'none of the above', not a forced softmax winner.

docs.name: SigLIP 2 (zero-shot image)
docs.blurb: SigLIP 2 (2025): multilingual sigmoid zero-shot — score an image against labels in ANY language, each an independent probability.
docs.unlocks: Multilingual multi-label tagging and honest moderation with independent per-label scores, all on-device.

docs.name: wav2vec2 (CTC speech-to-text)
docs.blurb: A CTC speech model: it labels every ~20ms frame with a character, then collapses the frames into text — a different architecture from Whisper, with no decoder and no hallucination.
docs.unlocks: Hallucination-free transcription plus per-word forced-alignment timings for free — the basis of captions, karaoke, and pronunciation feedback.

docs.name: RoBERTa GoEmotions (emotion)
docs.blurb: Score text across 28 emotions at once — multi-label sigmoid, not a single winner.
docs.unlocks: Private, nuanced emotion detection in the browser: emotion-based support routing, content mood tagging, and story emotion arcs, with nothing uploaded.

docs.name: Emotion — 7-class Ekman (DistilRoBERTa)
docs.blurb: Pick one of the 7 Ekman emotions — anger, disgust, fear, joy, neutral, sadness, surprise — with a real softmax that names a single winner.
docs.unlocks: Decisive, private emotion labels in the browser: one-tap message triage, single-emotion mood tags, and line-by-line story/lyric arcs, with a see-inside emotion wheel and nothing uploaded.

docs.name: Irony / sarcasm detection (Twitter-RoBERTa)
docs.blurb: Decide whether text is ironic/sarcastic — where the literal words and intended meaning diverge — with a Twitter-RoBERTa classifier.
docs.unlocks: A cheap on-device sarcasm guardrail: flag ironic text before a sentiment model trusts it, screen moderation edge cases, and chain irony + sentiment to recover the writer’s true meaning.

docs.name: FaceLandmarker (MediaPipe)
docs.blurb: Lay a 478-point mesh over any face and read its 52 live expression blendshapes plus head pose, from a photo or webcam.
docs.unlocks: On-device avatars and VTubing, attention/drowsiness cues, and beauty AR — ARKit-style face blendshapes in the browser, no native SDK.

docs.name: HandLandmarker (MediaPipe)
docs.blurb: Plot all 21 landmarks on every hand from a photo or live webcam, with handedness.
docs.unlocks: Touchless gesture UIs, sign-language tools, and air-drawing — real hand tracking on-device, no depth camera or SDK.

docs.name: PoseLandmarker (MediaPipe)
docs.blurb: Plot a 33-point body skeleton with per-joint visibility from a photo or live webcam.
docs.unlocks: On-device fitness form checks, rep counting, motion control, and pose-match games — no Kinect, no cloud vision API.

docs.name: GestureRecognizer (MediaPipe)
docs.blurb: Name the hand gesture (👍 thumbs up, ✌️ victory, ✋ open palm, ✊ fist…) with a confidence score, plus the 21 hand landmarks, from a photo or webcam.
docs.unlocks: Touchless UI controls, presentation and kiosk remotes, and gesture games — a named gesture becomes a ready-made button, on-device with no depth camera.

docs.name: FaceDetector (MediaPipe BlazeFace)
docs.blurb: Box every face with 6 keypoints and a confidence score, from a photo or webcam — a tiny ~230 KB BlazeFace model.
docs.unlocks: Client-side privacy blur, smart auto-crop, and identity-free face counting — a fast, free face-box primitive on-device, no cloud vision API.

docs.name: ViT (image classification)
docs.blurb: Name what is in an image across 1,000 ImageNet classes — with a confidence read-out.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline auto-tagging, upload triage, and confidence-gated routing in the browser.

docs.name: ConvNeXt (image classification)
docs.blurb: A pure ConvNet that matches transformers on ImageNet — with a ConvNeXt-vs-ViT head-to-head on the same image.
docs.unlocks: A stronger on-device tagger and cheap architecture ensembling (ConvNet + transformer) for robustness in the browser.

docs.name: ResNet-50 (image classification)
docs.blurb: The original 2015 deep residual CNN: name what is in an image across 1,000 ImageNet classes — with a ResNet-vs-ConvNeXt-vs-ViT backbone lineage race.
docs.unlocks: A dependable, battle-tested on-device tagger and a concrete architecture baseline (2015 CNN vs 2022 ConvNeXt vs ViT) in the browser.

docs.name: BEiT (image classification)
docs.blurb: BERT for images: a Vision Transformer pretrained self-supervised by masking image patches — with a BEiT-vs-supervised-ViT head-to-head.
docs.unlocks: Label-efficient, self-supervised image features and a different-error second opinion vs the supervised ViT, in the browser.

docs.name: MobileNetV2 (image classification)
docs.blurb: A ~3.5M-param mobile backbone: name what is in an image fast, with a real latency read-out and live webcam tagging.
docs.unlocks: Real-time on-device camera tagging and a cheap fast-prefilter → heavy-confirm cascade in the browser.

docs.name: Food-101 (dish classification)
docs.blurb: Name the dish across 101 Food-101 classes — a specialised food classifier with a confidence read-out.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline menu & nutrition tagging, food-diary logging, and dish de-duplication in the browser.

docs.name: Places365 scene classification
docs.blurb: Classify a photo's scene/place (kitchen, beach, office, …) across the 365 Places365 categories — a scene classifier, distinct from object/ImageNet classification.
docs.unlocks: Photo-library auto-organisation by place, indoor/outdoor gating, and context-aware apps from an on-device scene label.
docs.blockedReason: No Places365 scene classifier runs in the browser via transformers.js today. Verified 2026-07-19 (transformers.js 3.7.5, image-classification): there is no ONNX export with a config.json/preprocessor_config.json that transformers.js can load. Scanned the Places365/scene/indoor repos on HF: corenet-community/places365-224x224-vit-{base,large,huge} ship ONLY a raw PyTorch checkpoint (vit_base.pt) with no config.json and no ONNX; pratap4dev/resnet50-places365 and birder-project/rope_vit_reg4_b14_capi-places365 have no config.json and no ONNX (birder-native weights); litert-community/Places365-ResNet18-LiteRT ships ONLY places_fp16.tflite — a bare TFLite tensor graph, not a transformers.js ONNX and not a MediaPipe .task ImageClassifier bundle with embedded label metadata. The HF `onnx` tag filter returns no Places365/scene/indoor image classifier. The canonical MIT Places365 weights are Caffe/PyTorch only. Not shipped rather than relabel a generic ImageNet ViT as a scene classifier (ImageNet's 1,000 classes are objects, not the 365 scene categories). Unblocks when a Places365 model ships a browser-loadable ONNX (onnx-community/Xenova export with config + preprocessor), or when a metadata-bearing Places365 .tflite/.task is adopted via the MediaPipe ImageClassifier path.

docs.name: ViT-GPT2 (image captioning)
docs.blurb: Write a full-sentence description of any image, generated token by token in your browser.
docs.unlocks: On-device alt-text, searchable media libraries, and vision→language→sentiment pipelines.

docs.name: GOT-OCR-2.0
docs.tierRationale: distinct but the task already has built demos — build after the high tier.
docs.blurb: GOT-OCR-2.0 is a unified end-to-end OCR-2.0 model: whole-page dense/document/scene text, formatted (markdown/LaTeX) output. Distinct from TrOCR's single-line reader. Not runnable in the browser today — see blockedReason.
docs.unlocks: Whole-page structured OCR (document/receipt/screenshot/whiteboard → formatted text) — if it ran in-browser. Blocked by both a missing ONNX export and a runtime-class gap, not a weights-license gap.
docs.blockedReason: GOT-OCR-2.0 does not run via the shared Transformers.js path today (verified 2026-07-19), for three independent reasons. (1) NO ONNX export exists: onnx-community/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf returns HTTP 401/absent and the onnx-community org has no GOT repo; the canonical stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR2_0 (model_type 'GOT', arch GOTQwenForCausalLM) and stepfun-ai/GOT-OCR-2.0-hf (model_type 'got_ocr2', arch GotOcr2ForConditionalGeneration) ship only model.safetensors — no onnx/ folder. (2) The pinned Transformers.js 3.7.5 (webai.js TRANSFORMERS_URL) does not register the 'image-text-to-text' pipeline task at all: pipeline('image-text-to-text', ...) → 'Unsupported pipeline: image-text-to-text. Must be one of [...]'. (3) NO got_ocr2 model class is registered in Transformers.js 3.7.5 OR 4.2.0 (latest) — got_ocr2 is absent from both MODEL_FOR_IMAGE_TEXT_TO_TEXT_MAPPING_NAMES and MODEL_FOR_VISION_2_SEQ_MAPPING_NAMES — so even an -hf ONNX (if one existed) would throw 'Unsupported model type: got_ocr2'. Running GOT would require a bespoke ONNX Runtime Web pipeline + hand-wired Qwen tokenizer/generation loop off the shared createModelLoader path (inv12 / SKILL 4b). Recheck when an ONNX export ships AND Transformers.js adds a got_ocr2 class.

docs.name: GIT — Generative Image-to-Text
docs.blurb: GIT (GenerativeImage2Text) is a single generative transformer conditioned on image patches — captioning + VQA in one architecture, distinct from ViT-GPT2/BLIP encoder-decoders. Not runnable in the browser today — see blockedReason.
docs.unlocks: Image → generated caption (and VQA) from one unified transformer — if it ran in-browser. Blocked by both a missing ONNX export and a runtime-class gap.
docs.blockedReason: GIT (Microsoft GenerativeImage2Text; config model_type 'git', arch GitForCausalLM) does not run in the browser today (verified 2026-07-19). (1) NO ONNX export exists anywhere on HF: Xenova/git-base-coco and Xenova/git-base-vqav2 do not exist (HTTP 401 absent — Transformers.js load → 'Unauthorized access to file: .../Xenova/git-base-coco/resolve/main/config.json'); an HF search for browser-runnable git-base repos returns only PyTorch/flax checkpoints, none with an onnx/ folder. (2) The pinned Transformers.js 3.7.5 (and 4.2.0 latest) registers NO 'git' model class: 'git' is absent from MODEL_FOR_VISION_2_SEQ_MAPPING_NAMES, so pipeline('image-to-text', 'microsoft/git-base-coco') → 'Unsupported model type: git'. GIT's image-patch-conditioned single-transformer generation is architecturally distinct from ViT-GPT2 (built, VisionEncoderDecoder) and BLIP, but neither a runnable ONNX export nor a Transformers.js class exists. Recheck when an ONNX git export ships AND Transformers.js adds a GitForCausalLM class.

docs.name: PEGASUS XSum (summarization)
docs.tierRationale: distinct but the task already has built demos — build after the high tier.
docs.blurb: PEGASUS is the purpose-built abstractive-summarization architecture: its Gap-Sentence Generation pretraining masks whole salient sentences and makes the model regenerate them. XSum is the extreme single-sentence variant. Not runnable in the browser today — see blockedReason.
docs.unlocks: Extreme single-sentence abstractive summaries — if it ran in-browser. Blocked by a runtime gap, not a weights gap.
docs.blockedReason: No transformers.js support for the PEGASUS architecture in ANY version (verified 3.7.5, 4.0.0, 4.2.0-latest): 'Unsupported model type: pegasus'. Real canonical ONNX exports DO exist (onnx-community/pegasus_paraphrase-ONNX ships a full onnx/ folder with encoder + merged decoder across all dtypes; Rahmat82/pegasus_dialogue_summarization_onnx_quantized ships quantized encoder/decoder) but transformers.js registers no PegasusForConditionalGeneration / PegasusTokenizer, so both fail to instantiate. Running PEGASUS would require a bespoke ONNX Runtime Web pipeline + hand-wired SentencePiece tokenizer + generation loop, off the shared createModelLoader path (invariant 12 / SKILL 4b). Distinct from LongT5 (built), which is browser-runnable and reuses PEGASUS-style gap-sentence pretraining on a T5 backbone. Recheck when transformers.js adds a pegasus model class.

docs.blockedReason: Not browser-runnable (2026-07-19): duplicate of the blocked got-ocr2 family. No transformers.js class for model_type got_ocr2 in 3.7.5/4.2 and the image-text-to-text pipeline task does not exist in 3.7.5; stepfun-ai repos ship safetensors only (no onnx/). Re-check when transformers.js registers a GOT-OCR class.

docs.name: Multilingual MiniLM (cross-lingual embeddings)
docs.blurb: One 384-d embedding space shared across 50+ languages: a sentence and its translation land at nearly the same point, so cosine similarity measures meaning, not language.
docs.unlocks: Cross-language search, dedup and clustering from a single index — an English query finds its French or Japanese answer, with no per-language model and no translation round-trip.

docs.name: BGE-M3 (100-language dense embeddings)
docs.blurb: The dense-retrieval head of BAAI’s BGE-M3: one 1024-d space shared across 100+ languages with an 8192-token context window, so it embeds whole documents in any language. Larger than the other embedders (~570 MB q8) — the trade for breadth, length and quality.
docs.unlocks: Multilingual RAG and long-document search from a single on-device index — a query in any language finds the right passage in any other, no per-language model and no translation round-trip.

docs.name: ViLT (visual question answering)
docs.tierRationale: canonical model for a task with NO built demo yet, deployable license, browser-feasible.
docs.blurb: Patch+text co-transformer VQA — no ONNX build runs in the browser today.
docs.unlocks: Would answer free-text questions about an image with no external object detector.
docs.blockedReason: Not runnable in transformers.js 3.7.5 (verified 2026-07-19). Three compounding blockers: (1) transformers.js registers no `vilt` model class and `visual-question-answering` is not a supported pipeline task (`Unsupported pipeline: visual-question-answering`); (2) its processor is unimplemented (`Unknown feature_extractor_type: 'ViltFeatureExtractor'`); (3) no ONNX export exists — `Xenova/vilt-b32-finetuned-vqa` does not exist and `dandelin/vilt-b32-finetuned-vqa` ships only PyTorch weights (no `onnx/model_quantized.onnx`). Unblock: an upstream transformers.js ViltForQuestionAnswering class + processor AND a published ONNX export. Donut (document-question-answering) already covers browser VQA for documents.

docs.name: GLiNER (zero-shot NER by arbitrary labels)
docs.blurb: Generalist zero-shot NER: extract spans for entity types you invent at inference time.
docs.unlocks: Would enable user-defined-schema extraction (resume/invoice/contract fields, domain entities) with no fixed label set — if a browser runtime existed.
docs.blockedReason: transformers.js 3.7.5 registers NO `gliner` model class: pipeline('token-classification','onnx-community/gliner_small-v2.1') throws 'Unsupported model type: gliner', and the CDN bundle contains zero 'gliner' references. The onnx-community repos ship an ONNX export + config.json {model_type:'gliner'} but GLiNER's bidirectional span/entity-prompt pre/post-processing is not implemented as a transformers.js class (same for onnx-community/gliner-multitask-large-v0.5). Verified 2026-07-19 in headless Chrome. Unblocks when transformers.js adds a GLiNER model class/processor.

docs.name: DETR panoptic (segmentation)
docs.blurb: Panoptic segmentation: split a scene into per-instance object masks (things) plus labelled background regions (stuff), each colour-coded.
docs.unlocks: On-device instance-accurate cut-outs, scene inventory, and region-level redaction with nothing uploaded.

docs.name: MobileCLIP (zero-shot image)
docs.blurb: Apple MobileCLIP-S0: fast on-device zero-shot image classification — rank an image against any labels, with a mobile-tuned vision tower.
docs.unlocks: Fast on-device tagging, moderation triage, and real-time camera classification at a fraction of a full ViT-B/16 CLIP encode time.

docs.name: Facial expression recognition
docs.blurb: Read a facial expression on-device across 7 emotions — from your camera or a photo — with a confidence + calibration read-out.
docs.unlocks: Private, consented affective UX in the browser: reaction capture, sentiment-aware flows, and expression-over-time — with the camera frame never leaving the device.

docs.name: Spoken language identification (MMS-LID)
docs.blurb: Identify the spoken language of audio out of 126 — from the mic, a file, or a sample — the audio-first cousin of text language detection.
docs.unlocks: The router step before every multilingual speech pipeline: detect the language on-device, then send the audio to the right ASR / translator with no server round-trip.

docs.name: YOLOv10 (NMS-free detection)
docs.blurb: Real-time object detection with no Non-Max-Suppression — one pass returns already-deduplicated boxes.
docs.unlocks: Fast, tiny on-device detection for live camera, counting and inventory, with nothing uploaded.

docs.name: XLM-RoBERTa (multilingual fill-mask)
docs.blurb: Fill a <mask> in ~100 languages with one model sharing a single 250k-token vocabulary.
docs.unlocks: One on-device model that serves multilingual autocomplete, knowledge probing and cloze tasks worldwide.

docs.name: OuteTTS (LLM text-to-speech)
docs.blurb: LLM-based TTS — a Qwen2 model generates discrete audio tokens that the WavTokenizer neural codec decodes into speech.
docs.unlocks: Private, controllable narration where the same sampling knobs as a chat LLM shape the voice — no API keys, no per-character billing.

docs.name: ViTPose (pose estimation)
docs.blurb: A plain Vision Transformer that estimates 17 COCO body keypoints from per-joint heatmaps — a top-down pose estimator, distinct from MediaPipe PoseLandmarker.
docs.unlocks: On-device human pose estimation with a transformer: fitness form checks, joint-angle analysis, stick-figure puppets, and detector→pose for whole scenes — no depth camera, no cloud.

docs.name: Multilingual zero-shot image (Jina-CLIP v2)
docs.blurb: Match an image to text labels written in ANY of 89 languages — the same concept in English, Spanish, Chinese, or Arabic scores alike. (AltCLIP has no Transformers.js class; jina-clip-v2 delivers the capability.)
docs.unlocks: Global, on-device image tagging, moderation, and cross-lingual search with one model instead of one per locale.

docs.name: Qwen2.5 0.5B on the CPU (WASM)
docs.blurb: A capable 0.5B instruct LLM streaming on the CPU via WebAssembly — no WebGPU required.
docs.unlocks: Client-side chat, extraction, and RAG on ANY device with a WASM engine — no GPU, no server, no key.

docs.name: Kosmos-2 (grounded image-to-text)
docs.tierRationale: distinct but the task already has built demos — build after the high tier.
docs.blurb: Kosmos-2 is a grounded VLM: it generates a caption AND grounds noun phrases to bounding boxes. Not runnable in the browser today — see blockedReason.
docs.unlocks: Would enable grounded captioning + referring-expression localization (describe-and-locate, a11y alt-text with regions) — if a browser runtime existed. Blocked by both a missing ONNX export and a runtime-class gap.
docs.blockedReason: Kosmos-2 (config model_type kosmos-2, arch Kosmos2ForConditionalGeneration) does not run in the browser today (verified 2026-07-19). (1) NO transformers.js model class exists in ANY version: mod.Kosmos2ForConditionalGeneration is undefined in the pinned 3.7.5, and the CDN dist bundles for 3.7.5, 4.2.0 and latest contain ZERO kosmos references (grep), while florence2/sam2/idefics/smolvlm ARE present — so pipeline("image-to-text", "microsoft/kosmos-2-patch14-224") maps to no class. (2) NO browser-loadable ONNX export exists: Xenova/kosmos-2-patch14-224 does not exist (HF 401 -> transformers.js load throws "Unauthorized access to file: .../tokenizer_config.json"); the canonical microsoft/kosmos-2-patch14-224 ships only model.safetensors + pytorch_model.bin (no onnx/ folder); no onnx-community Kosmos-2 export exists. Kosmos-2s grounded caption-plus-boxes output is architecturally distinct from Florence-2 (built, WebGPU) and BLIP, but neither a runnable ONNX export nor a transformers.js class exists. Recheck when an ONNX Kosmos-2 export ships AND transformers.js registers a Kosmos2ForConditionalGeneration class.

docs.name: ByT5-small (byte-level seq2seq)
docs.blurb: A token-free byte-level seq2seq model: it reads raw UTF-8 bytes instead of sub-word tokens, so every script, emoji, and typo is handled identically and nothing is ever out-of-vocabulary.
docs.unlocks: Truly multilingual, noise-robust text processing with no vocabulary to ship — plus a legible demo of how tokenization actually works.

docs.name: InternVL2.5-1B (vision-language)
docs.blurb: A compact InternVL vision-language model (ViT vision encoder + pixel-shuffle projector + InternLM/Qwen2 decoder) for image question-answering and document reasoning.
docs.unlocks: Would add a distinct on-device VLM family (alongside Qwen2-VL / PaliGemma / SmolVLM / Moondream / nanoLLaVA) — but is not browser-runnable today.
docs.blockedReason: Transformers.js registers no InternVL model class. Verified 2026-07-19: grepping the shared pinned build @huggingface/transformers@3.7.5 AND the latest @4.2.0 dist/transformers.web.js (and src/tokenizers.js) finds no InternVL* class, no image-text-to-text auto-map for model_type "internvl" (only substrings "Internal"/"internally"); the config of the only browser ONNX exports (third-party BricksDisplay/InternVL3-1B-onnx / -2B-onnx) declares model_type "internvl", architecture "InternVLForConditionalGeneration" — so a dedicated-class or pipeline load throws "Unsupported model type: internvl" (same failure class as pegasus/gliner/got-ocr2). No onnx-community InternVL repo exists (search + resolve of onnx-community/InternVL2_5-1B-MPO/-ONNX and onnx-community/InternVL3-1B-ONNX all 401). The upstream OpenGVLab/InternVL2_5-1B-MPO is safetensors-only. Unblocks when Transformers.js adds an InternVL model class + processor (dynamic tiling, pixel-shuffle, interleaved image tokens) and a canonical onnx-community ONNX export ships.

docs.name: AI-generated image detector (Swin)
docs.blurb: Screen an image locally for whether it looks AI-generated or a real photograph — an AI-generated vs real probability with the confidence behind it, framed honestly as an imperfect signal, not proof of origin.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline provenance triage: route likely-AI uploads to human review with no API bill and no image ever leaving the device.

docs.name: Model2Vec static embeddings (potion-base-8M)
docs.blurb: Turn text into vectors with a distilled lookup table — no transformer, no attention, just gather-and-average. Microsecond-scale embeddings on a plain CPU for semantic search and clustering.
docs.unlocks: Blazing-fast, tiny-footprint embeddings: index a huge corpus or re-embed on every keystroke on-device, where an attention model like MiniLM would be too slow.

docs.name: Mask2Former (universal segmentation)
docs.blurb: Universal segmentation — one masked-attention architecture for semantic, instance AND panoptic — distinct from SegFormer (semantic) and DETR-panoptic.
docs.unlocks: A single model for every segmentation task via mask classification instead of per-pixel labels.
docs.blockedReason: No transformers.js model class exists for Mask2Former in ANY version. Verified 2026-07-19: the MODEL_FOR_UNIVERSAL_SEGMENTATION_MAPPING registers only ['detr','DetrForSegmentation'] and ['maskformer','MaskFormerForInstanceSegmentation'] in both the shared 3.7.5 pin AND the newest 4.2.0 (latest) — only a Mask2FormerImageProcessor ships, no Mask2FormerModel/Mask2FormerForUniversalSegmentation, so loading model_type 'mask2former' fails with 'Unsupported model type: mask2former'. Separately, NO browser ONNX export exists to load even if the class did: the canonical facebook/mask2former-* repos are safetensors/PyTorch only (0 .onnx files; config model_type confirmed 'mask2former'), and neither Xenova nor onnx-community publish any mask2former ONNX repo (Xenova/mask2former-swin-tiny-coco-panoptic does not exist → HF 404). The version-pin escape hatch (as used for SAM2's Sam2Model in 4.2.0) does not help because 4.2.0 also lacks the class. Not shipped rather than mislabel MaskFormer (a different architecture, which IS runnable) as Mask2Former. Unblocks when transformers.js registers a Mask2Former universal-segmentation model class AND an ONNX export (via Xenova/onnx-community) ships.

docs.name: Snowflake Arctic-Embed-S (retrieval embeddings)
docs.blurb: Snowflake retrieval-tuned embedder — CLS pooling with an asymmetric query prefix, 384-d vectors.
docs.unlocks: First-stage retrieval and private RAG in the tab, with the query-prefix trick shown A/B and Arctic/GTE/MiniLM compared.

docs.name: Grammar correction (grammar-synthesis-small)
docs.blurb: A T5 seq2seq fine-tuned to rewrite ungrammatical, typo-ridden, or ESL English into clean prose, with a word-level diff.
docs.unlocks: A private, offline proofreader — regenerates the whole sentence, robust to ASR transcripts and heavy errors; no API key or cloud.

docs.name: GTE-large (1024-d retrieval embeddings)
docs.blurb: The heavyweight GTE embedder: a BERT-large encoder that emits 1024-d sentence vectors with higher MTEB retrieval quality than GTE-small — same mean-pool, no-prefix recipe, a much larger download.
docs.unlocks: Server-grade retrieval quality entirely client-side: sharper semantic search, near-duplicate detection and RAG shortlists where a mis-ranked hit is expensive — pick this tier when accuracy beats the ~337 MB/latency cost.

docs.name: Question generation (text2text seq2seq)
docs.blurb: Given a passage (and optionally a highlighted answer span), generate reading-comprehension questions — a distinct text2text use from summarize/translate.
docs.unlocks: Auto-generated quizzes, flashcards and study aids, and Socratic question chains — from any passage, on-device.
docs.blockedReason: No dedicated question-generation model ships a Transformers.js-3.7.5-loadable ONNX that produces usable output (verified 2026-07-19). (1) The classic QG checkpoints have NO ONNX export at all: valhalla/t5-small-qg-hl, valhalla/t5-small-e2e-qg, mrm8488/t5-base-finetuned-question-generation-ap, iarfmoose/t5-base-question-generator, and the lmqg/*, p208p2002/*, allenai and research-backup QG families are PyTorch/safetensors-only (0 .onnx files each). (2) The only dedicated QG model with a v3-loadable merged-decoder ONNX is dantedgp/flan-t5-small-finetuned-question-generation, and its export is DEGENERATE: it ships ONLY quantized ONNX (no fp32 fallback) and every decoding config (greedy, beam x4, sampling, repetition_penalty/no_repeat_ngram) returns repeated-token garbage ('The The The...', 'About About About...'); the identical harness on Xenova/flan-t5-small q8 returns real questions, proving the harness is correct and the dantedgp quantized weights are broken. (3) A genuine -qg-hl model with the <hl>/<sep> answer-span mechanism exists in ONNX — WiseIntelligence/t5-base-question-generator-Optimum-ONNX (and its -Quantized-AVX2 sibling, ~422 MB) — but both use the pre-v3 SEPARATE-decoder layout (decoder_model.onnx + decoder_with_past_model.onnx, no decoder_model_merged.onnx). Transformers.js 3.7.5 hardcodes decoder_model_merged for T5ForConditionalGeneration generation (verified in dist source: sessions['decoder_model_merged']), so it errors 'Could not locate decoder_model_merged.onnx' and cannot load them. Relabeling the already-built Xenova/flan-t5-small instruction demo as a dedicated QG model is disallowed. Unblocks when a real QG model ships a Transformers.js-v3-loadable (decoder_model_merged) ONNX with non-degenerate output — e.g. a Xenova/onnx-community re-export of a t5-qg-hl checkpoint.

docs.name: Age estimation
docs.blurb: Estimate APPARENT age on-device across 9 buckets, folded into one expected-age number — an imperfect, bias-carrying signal, never identity or verification.
docs.unlocks: Private, consented age-aware UX in the browser: signal-not-verdict defaults, faceless audience analytics, and calibration-gated decisions — with the frame never leaving the device.

docs.name: Paraphrase generation (T5)
docs.blurb: Rewrite a sentence while preserving meaning — a dedicated T5-base paraphraser that produces several diverse re-wordings via sampling, side-by-side with similarity read-outs.
docs.unlocks: A local rewrite engine: copywriting A/B variants, editor reword buttons, and dataset augmentation — no API key, no per-call bill, no text leaving the device.

docs.name: TAPAS (table question answering)
docs.blurb: TAPAS answers natural-language questions over a TABLE — selecting cells and applying an aggregation (SUM/COUNT/AVERAGE) — a structured-data QA task distinct from free-text extractive QA. Not runnable in the browser today — see blockedReason.
docs.unlocks: Spreadsheet / receipt / report Q&A on-device (ask a table a question, get an answer + the cells + the aggregation) — if it ran in the browser. Blocked by a missing model class AND a missing pipeline task AND a missing ONNX export, not a license gap.
docs.blockedReason: TAPAS does not run via the shared Transformers.js path today (verified 2026-07-19), for three independent reasons. (1) NO tapas model class is registered in Transformers.js 3.7.5 (webai.js TRANSFORMERS_URL pin) OR 4.2.0 (latest): grepping every dist bundle (transformers.js / .min.js / .web.js / .node.cjs) for 'tapas', 'TapasFor', 'TapasForQuestionAnswering' returns ZERO matches in both versions, so loading would throw 'Unsupported model type: tapas'. (2) The 'table-question-answering' pipeline task does not exist in Transformers.js 3.7.5 or 4.2.0 — grep of the bundles for 'table-question-answering' returns zero matches (the pipeline registry has question-answering, text-classification, token-classification, zero-shot-classification, etc., but no table variant); pipeline('table-question-answering', ...) → 'Unsupported pipeline'. (3) NO browser ONNX export exists: Xenova/tapas-base-finetuned-wtq → HTTP 401/absent, onnx-community/tapas-base-finetuned-wtq → HTTP 401/absent, Xenova/tapas-base → 401; only google/tapas-base-finetuned-wtq exists and it is PyTorch/safetensors-only with no onnx/ folder. TAPAS also needs its own TapasTokenizer/TapasForQuestionAnswering table encoding (row/column/segment/rank embeddings + a cell-selection + aggregation head) that Transformers.js has no processor for. Running it would require a bespoke ONNX Runtime Web pipeline plus a hand-written TAPAS table tokenizer off the shared createModelLoader path. Recheck when a TAPAS ONNX export ships AND Transformers.js adds a tapas class + table-question-answering pipeline. Do NOT relabel a free-text extractive QA model (e.g. DistilBERT SQuAD) as table QA.

docs.name: Multilingual BERT NER
docs.blurb: Tag people, organizations, locations and dates in ten languages with one multilingual BERT model.
docs.unlocks: One on-device NER pipeline for Arabic, German, English, Spanish, French, Italian, Latvian, Dutch, Portuguese and Chinese — cross-border redaction, extraction and cross-lingual entity linking, nothing leaving the tab.

docs.name: GTE-ModernBERT (long-context embeddings)
docs.blurb: A 768-d embedder on the ModernBERT backbone: an 8192-token context, CLS pooling, top-MTEB retrieval.
docs.unlocks: Long-document semantic search, dedup, clustering and retrieve-then-rerank pipelines that read whole documents in one 8192-token context, all in the browser.

docs.name: Speech Commands (keyword spotting)
docs.blurb: Recognise 35 short spoken keywords (yes/no/up/down/stop/go and the digits) from the mic or a clip, on-device.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline wake-word spotting and hands-free voice control — no key, no bill, no audio leaving the page.

docs.name: YOLO11 (dense-head detection)
docs.blurb: Ultralytics newest YOLO — accurate dense-head detection whose 8400 raw candidates you dedupe with live, adjustable Non-Max-Suppression.
docs.unlocks: The classic detection pipeline made legible: tune NMS on a photo or a live camera, on-device, nothing uploaded.

docs.name: Spam detector (tanaos DistilBERT)
docs.blurb: Classify a message as spam or not-spam, with a triage inbox and the spam-signal words.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline inbox/comment/DM triage — junk filtered client-side, no message leaves the device.

docs.name: BART-large-CNN (summarization)
docs.blurb: The canonical news abstractive summarizer — higher quality than DistilBART, larger download.
docs.unlocks: High-quality on-device TL;DRs of articles and reports, streamed as they generate — no API key or per-call billing.

docs.name: T5-base (task-prefix seq2seq)
docs.blurb: The ORIGINAL T5: one text-to-text model whose task is chosen entirely by an explicit prefix — translate English to German:, summarize:, cola sentence: (grammaticality), stsb sentence1:… sentence2:… (similarity). Change the prefix and the same weights do a different job.
docs.unlocks: A single 250 MB download that translates, summarises, grammar-checks, and scores sentence similarity on-device — and a legible demo of the multi-task-via-prefix format that underlies modern LLMs.

docs.name: CodeBERTa (code fill-mask)
docs.license: unlicensed (HuggingFace CodeBERTa; CodeSearchNet data MIT; weights public + ungated)
docs.blurb: A masked language model from Hugging Face’s CodeBERT family, pre-trained on source CODE (CodeSearchNet: Go, Java, JavaScript, PHP, Python, Ruby). Put a <mask> in a snippet and it predicts the missing code token with the exact logits over its 52k code vocabulary.
docs.unlocks: Offline, bidirectional code-token prediction — in-editor identifier/API-name hints, naming help, and anomaly signals — with no server and no prompt engineering.

docs.name: DialoGPT small (conversational)
docs.blurb: A GPT-2-based chatbot trained on 147M Reddit exchanges. Chat with it locally and see its one trick: the whole conversation is fed back as a single sequence with each turn separated by the end-of-text token.
docs.unlocks: A legible view of how a chatbot really works (EOS-joined history + text completion); offline private conversational filler; quick-reply suggestions; a reply stage for local pipelines.

docs.name: mBART-50 many-to-many (translation)
docs.blurb: Translate directly between any pair of 50 languages with one multilingual denoising-pretrained seq2seq model. The target language is chosen by a forced beginning-of-sequence token.
docs.unlocks: Private, offline many-to-many translation across 50 languages from a single model, with the forced target-language token laid bare.

license: unlicensed (HuggingFace CodeBERTa; CodeSearchNet data MIT; weights public + ungated)
